_comment: Full titled references for the benchmark-page bibliography. Keyed by <first-author-token><year> (the same key the page uses to dedupe citations). The page prefers the entry here; any key not listed falls back to the short 'Author (year) Journal' citation from the benchmark data. Edit freely — entries flagged in chat as 'verify' should be checked against the PDF.
bahill1975: Bahill AT, Clark MR, Stark L (1975). The main sequence, a tool for studying human eye movements. Mathematical Biosciences 24:191–204.
robinson1975: Robinson DA (1975). Oculomotor control signals. In: Lennerstrand G, Bach-y-Rita P (eds), Basic Mechanisms of Ocular Motility and Their Clinical Implications. Pergamon Press, pp. 337–374.
smit1990: Smit AC, Van Opstal AJ, Van Gisbergen JAM (1990). Component stretching in fast and slow oblique saccades in the human. Experimental Brain Research 81:325–343.
van1985: Van Gisbergen JAM, Van Opstal AJ, Schoenmakers JJM (1985). Experimental test of two models for the generation of oblique saccades. Experimental Brain Research 57:321–336.
becker1979: Becker W, Jürgens R (1979). An analysis of the saccadic system by means of double step stimuli. Vision Research 19:967–983.
kapoula1986: Kapoula Z, Robinson DA, Hain TC (1986). Motion of the eye immediately after a saccade. Experimental Brain Research 61:386–394.
scudder2002: Scudder CA, Kaneko CRS, Fuchs AF (2002). The brainstem burst generator for saccadic eye movements: a modern synthesis. Experimental Brain Research 142:439–462.
raphan1979: Raphan T, Matsuo V, Cohen B (1979). Velocity storage in the vestibulo-ocular reflex arc (VOR). Experimental Brain Research 35:229–248.
cohen1977: Cohen B, Matsuo V, Raphan T (1977). Quantitative analysis of the velocity characteristics of optokinetic nystagmus and optokinetic after-nystagmus. Journal of Physiology 270:321–344.
laurens2011: Laurens J, Angelaki DE (2011). The functional significance of velocity storage and its dependence on gravity. Experimental Brain Research 210:407–422.
boff1986: Boff KR, Kaufman L, Thomas JP (eds) (1986). Handbook of Perception and Human Performance. Wiley, New York.
denise1988: Denise P, Darlot C, Droulez J, Cohen B, Berthoz A (1988). Motion perceptions induced by off-vertical axis rotation (OVAR) at small angles of tilt. Experimental Brain Research 67:604–611.
wood2002: Wood SJ (2002). Human otolith–ocular reflexes during off-vertical axis rotation: effect of frequency on tilt–translation ambiguity and motion sickness. Neuroscience Letters 323:41–44.
mayne1974: Mayne R (1974). A systems concept of the vestibular organs. In: Kornhuber HH (ed), Handbook of Sensory Physiology, Vol. VI/2: Vestibular System. Springer, pp. 493–580.
lisberger1985: Lisberger SG, Westbrook LE (1985). Properties of visual inputs that initiate horizontal smooth pursuit eye movements in monkeys. Journal of Neuroscience 5:1662–1673.
rashbass1961: Rashbass C, Westheimer G (1961). Disjunctive eye movements. Journal of Physiology 159:339–360.
carl1987: Carl JR, Gellman RS (1987). Human smooth pursuit: stimulus-dependent responses. Journal of Neurophysiology 57:1446–1463.
lisberger1981: Lisberger SG, Evinger C, Johanson GW, Fuchs AF (1981). Relationship between eye acceleration and retinal image velocity during foveal smooth pursuit in man and monkey. Journal of Neurophysiology 46:229–249.
mays1984: Mays LE (1984). Neural control of vergence eye movements: convergence and divergence neurons in midbrain. Journal of Neurophysiology 51:1091–1108.
hung1986: Hung GK, Semmlow JL, Ciuffreda KJ (1986). A dual-mode dynamic model of the vergence eye movement system. IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering 33:1021–1028.
del2017: Del Águila-Carrasco AJ, Marín-Franch I, Bernal-Molina P, Esteve-Taboada JJ, Kruger PB, Montés-Micó R, López-Gil N (2017). Accommodation responds to optical vergence and not defocus blur alone. Investigative Ophthalmology & Visual Science 58:1758–1763.
read2022: Read SA, Buehren T, Collins MJ (2022). Predictive accommodation responses. Journal of Vision (verify title/volume).
zee1992: Zee DS, FitzGibbon EJ, Optican LM (1992). Saccade-vergence interactions in humans. Journal of Neurophysiology 68:1624–1641.
collewijn1988: Collewijn H, Erkelens CJ, Steinman RM (1988). Binocular co-ordination of human horizontal saccadic eye movements. Journal of Physiology 404:157–182.
schor1979: Schor CM (1979). The relationship between fusional vergence eye movements and fixation disparity. Vision Research 19:1359–1367.
morgan1944: Morgan MW (1944). The clinical aspects of accommodation and convergence. American Journal of Optometry & Archives of the American Academy of Optometry 21:301–313.
hung1992: Hung GK (1992). Adaptation model of accommodation and vergence. Ophthalmic & Physiological Optics 12:319–326.
schor1988: Schor CM (1988). Imbalanced adaptation of accommodation and vergence produces opposite extremes of the AC/A and CA/C ratios. American Journal of Optometry & Physiological Optics 65:341–348.
stark1965: Stark L, Takahashi Y, Zames G (1965). Nonlinear servoanalysis of human lens accommodation. IEEE Transactions on Systems Science and Cybernetics SSC-1:75–83.
paige1991: Paige GD, Tomko DL (1991). Eye movement responses to linear head motion in the squirrel monkey. I. Basic characteristics. Journal of Neurophysiology 65:1170–1182.
angelaki2001: Angelaki DE, Hess BJM (2001). Direction of heading and vestibular control of binocular eye movements. Vision Research 41:3215–3228.
rolfs2009: Rolfs M (2009). Microsaccades: small steps on a long way. Vision Research 49:2415–2441.
tweed1998: Tweed D, Haslwanter T, Fetter M (1998). Optimizing gaze control in three dimensions. Science 281:1363–1366.
van1993: Van Rijn LJ, Van den Berg AV (1993). Binocular eye orientation during fixations: Listing's law extended to include eye vergence. Vision Research 33:691–708.
listing1854: Listing JB (1854). Listing's law of ocular torsion (as reported by Ruete; later formalized by Helmholtz, Handbuch der physiologischen Optik, 1867).
